scene_descriptions: Dramatic camera swoop on Turia bike lane — instant motion hook
Wide view of Rio Turia park with bike path, trees, and people strolling
Immersive cycling POV through tree-lined Turia path, handlebars visible
Jardin Real with palm trees, bench, and cyclist — hidden garden feel
Stone staircase entrance to a Turia park zone — architectural transition
Palau de la Musica ramp panorama sweeping from houses down to the park
Park with tall building, palm trees, and statue — Benimaclet greenery
Calm park scene with trees and street — quiet neighborhood breathing room
Black office chair found on sidewalk while cycling — street-find surprise moment
Close-up of discarded office chair next to trash bin — the item reveal
Park bench with lamp post — calm cycling CTA setting
Palm-tree-lined street ride-away — cinematic closing shot
thematic_elements: green urban corridor — Valencia's Turia riverbed converted into parkland
cycling as discovery tool — seeing the city from handlebars
garden variety — from manicured Jardin Real to wild Turia stretches
street-find surprise — functional office chair abandoned on the sidewalk
palm trees as visual motif — recurring across parks and streets
community spaces — benches, paths, people walking, shared greenery
60-second time pressure — rapid tour through multiple parks
suggested_natural_phrases: lo que esconden los parques de Valencia
esto estaba tirado en la acera
de parque en parque en 60 segundos
givore_mention_guideline: This is broad Tier 1/Tier 2 content (cycling POV + barrio guide). The street-find chair provides one natural Givore mention opportunity — keep it casual, e.g. 'por cierto, tengo una app para esto' or weave into the CTA. Rest of the video should be pure park discovery with no app push. Target: 70-80% pure engagement, 20-30% casual mention via the item moment.
